threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You start saying 'the Tube' instead of 'the underground' and can't remember the last time you walked barefoot on a proper lawn.
Late on a Friday, saw someone serenading a group at the back of the 133. The random karaoke made the journey feel like a mini night out.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing football on the street, dodging trams, it hit me—this is home, chaotic and alive.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner shop that doubles as a vinyl record store. The mix of beats and kebabs is uniquely Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows that the Civic Center BART station isn’t just the hub for transit; it’s the unofficial smoke detector for the city's drama.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a bike messenger dodging Muni as it swayed to a halt. A perfect San Francisco dance on the streets—chaos meets cool.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the quiet streets in the early morning are always peaceful—try 8 AM when the bakery line starts forming. It's chaos!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newbies; they’re the ones standing in front of the mural ‘tapping to enter’ and fumbling with the doorbell.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still hesitate to cross the road when the red man is flashing, thinking it means something.
Caught a bloke singing 'Wonderwall' at 3 AM on the 196. Must've been a karaoke night gone wrong; the bus driver was in on it, laughing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard the guy at the corner shop arguing with a customer about the best döner in town, I realized I’d found my spot.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM, then stumble across a pop-up art exhibit in an old warehouse—talk about late-night culture!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell the regulars by how they react to BART breaking down. If they roll their eyes and keep scrolling their phone, they’re locals.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 Fulton this morning, and a woman was explaining the history of the Tenderloin to her dog. The dog seemed more engaged than most tourists.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the squirrels in Parc Guell are just cute; locals know they can be aggressive when food is involved. Don’t feed them!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their fascination with the Gothic architecture, snapping photos like they’ve never seen a building older than 100 years.
